message:
chore: Update Beads issue tracker database state

This commit updates the Beads dependency-aware issue tracker's JSONL database
files, which serve as the source of truth for issue state in version control.

Files Updated:
==============

beads.base.jsonl & beads.base.meta.json
----------------------------------------
Base layer database containing the foundational issue state. The .base files
represent the committed state of the issue database before any local
modifications. These files are updated when changes are committed/synced.

Metadata includes:
- Database schema version
- Last sync timestamp
- Branch tracking information
- Conflict resolution state

beads.left.jsonl & beads.left.meta.json
----------------------------------------
Left layer database representing the current working state of issues,
including any local modifications that haven't been pushed to the base
layer yet. This enables a two-layer architecture for collaborative
issue tracking with built-in merge/sync capabilities.

issues.jsonl
------------
Main issue database in JSON Lines format. Each line represents a single
issue with complete metadata:
- Issue ID, title, status, priority
- Dependencies and blockers
- Time estimates and progress tracking
- Labels, assignees, timestamps
- Rich text descriptions and comments

This format allows for:
1. Efficient append-only operations
2. Line-by-line git diffs (human-readable issue changes)
3. Merge conflict resolution at issue granularity
4. Compatibility with standard unix tools (grep, awk, jq)

Beads Architecture Benefits:
============================
- Dependency-aware task scheduling (topological sorting)
- Distributed collaboration without central server
- Git-native storage (no external DB required)
- Offline-first workflow
- Automatic conflict detection and resolution
- Rich query language for issue filtering

These files are explicitly allowed by .beads/.gitignore which states:
"Keep JSONL exports and config (source of truth for git)"

This update captures the current state of project issues and their
dependency relationships, providing a snapshot of work planning and
progress tracking at this point in the project timeline.
